  "new_string": "## Language warnings (English pitch copy)

**Avoid — these English terms imply regulated or medical-device territory:**

- **"Diagnose" / "diagnostic" / "early detection of dementia"** — under MDR + EU AI Act, software with diagnostic intent is a medical device, almost certainly high-risk. We don't diagnose. Use "document", "capture", "surface", "support". The Dutch equivalents *diagnostiek* and *vroegsignalering dementie* are equally regulated.
- **"Advance directive" / "living will"** — these are legal artefacts. The Dutch equivalent is *wilsverklaring*, owned by KNMG ([KNMG](https://www.knmg.nl/actueel/dossiers/levenseinde-2/zelfbeschikking/wilsverklaring)). Our output is never a directive. Say "ACP topics covered" or "future-care wishes captured", not "advance directive generated".
- **"Decision capacity" / "competence to consent"** — clinical-legal judgement made by a physician. The Dutch equivalent is *wilsbekwaamheid* ([Richtlijnendatabase](https://richtlijnendatabase.nl/richtlijn/dementie_en_lichte_cognitieve_stoornissen_mild_cognitive_impairment_mci/dementie_als_comorbiditeit_in_het_ziekenhuis/wils_oordeelsbekwaamheid_en_behandelbeperking.html)). We never assess it.
- **"Treatment plan" / "care plan"** — that is the ECD's *zorgleefplan* / *zorgplan*, owned by the provider organisation. We feed *into* the care plan; we are not it. Use "care passport", "preference record", "personhood profile".
- **"Triage" / "risk score" / "red flag"** — implies decision support. Use "signal", "things to watch", "for clinical review".
- **"Legal representative" / "guardian" / "appoint a proxy"** — court-assigned roles in NL (*mentor*, *curator*, *bewindvoerder*). We never appoint or replace them.
- **"Diagnostic-grade" / "clinical-grade"** — language that implies regulatory clearance we do not have.
- **"Replaces the case manager / nurse / family"** — adversarial framing of human roles. Always say *augments*, *supports*, *prepares*.

**Align with — these English terms have established meaning in person-centered dementia care; use them rather than inventing alternatives:**

- **"Person-centered care" (PCC)** — Kitwood's term, RCN/NICE-blessed, the global standard. Don't say "patient-centric AI" or "personalised care AI". Say person-centered.
- **"Personhood"** — Kitwood's specific construct; signals theoretical literacy.
- **"Life story" / "life-story work"** — established intervention name in nursing literature; the Dutch *levensboek* is the closest analog and Caren's *Mijn verhaal* is its digital placeholder. Use "life story" in pitch copy.
- **"This is Me"** — the Alzheimer's Society UK leaflet, RCN-endorsed since 2010. Name-check it explicitly: we are the digital, voice-native, longitudinal evolution.
- **"Care preferences" / "preference profile"** — neutral, accepted by ECD vendors and clinicians; doesn't claim clinical authority.
- **"Advance care planning (ACP) topics"** — international standard term; matches the Dutch *proactieve zorgplanning* themes one-to-one. Say "ACP topics covered", not "ACP completed".
- **"Caregiver" (formal) / "informal caregiver" / "family carer"** — distinguish professional from family. The Dutch *mantelzorger* maps to "informal caregiver"; do not collapse them.
- **"Case manager" (dementia case manager)** — direct translation of *casemanager dementie*; the role exists in UK/IE/AU and is recognisable.
- **"Source-cited" / "evidence-backed" / "with provenance"** — our trust-and-safety framing; reuse consistently.
- **"Handover" / "transition of care"** — established failure-mode language; use it to anchor the pain."
}

**(b) The gap — verbatim, pitch-ready:**

- The ECD captures the diagnosis, the medication, and the schedule. It does not capture how she likes to be approached, what calms her, or what her own word for her husband is. That layer lives in a head, a sticky note, or a paper *ik-boekje*.
- The Dutch system already has the right intent — Zorgstandaard Dementie, Waardigheid en Trots, Nedap "Mijn verhaal" — but the data is unstructured prose, mostly family-authored after the fact, refreshed once if at all, and not searchable at the point of care.
- National guidance prescribes six ACP themes (dagelijks plezier, dagelijkse zorg, behandeling, omgaan met dementie/levenseinde, financiën, wonen). There is no tool that listens to the patient over time and fills those six wedges with their own voice.
- Tacit knowledge — the "magic hands" of an experienced verzorgende — is documented in the academic literature as ungeschreven en niet-overdraagbaar; when the nurse leaves or the patient transfers, it is gone. This is not a bug in the ECD; it is the boundary of what the ECD was designed to hold.
- Our wedge: capture this layer in the patient's own voice, before they can't articulate it, and make it queryable for any caregiver who shows up. We do not replace the ECD. We feed it the column it doesn't have.

**(c) Vocabulary recs — N/A** (per course-correction, dropped the Dutch-vocabulary table; the brief now ships an English-language warnings/alignment section instead, with brief Dutch parentheticals where the source term matters).

**(d) The surprise**

Nedap's "Mijn verhaal" is not a placeholder — it is a shipped feature inside Caren and Ons Dossier today, sitting under the client's name, owned by the client, surfaced to professionals. It is currently a free-text/photo blob, family-authored, and at last check editing was disabled — but the *slot* for the patient's voice already exists in the ECD of the dominant Dutch vendor. That changes our framing: we are "the engine that fills Mijn verhaal with the patient's actual voice and structure," not "we invented the patient-voice field." It also makes Nedap our biggest single platform risk — they could extend Mijn verhaal themselves — which argues for moving fast on a Caren-export integration. Casemanagers already know the concept; we don't have to teach it, only the upgrade path.

## What we're building

1	# Problem Statement
2	
3	People with dementia and other degenerative diseases can lose the ability to explain their wishes, defend their interests, and make medical decisions for themselves. For people with close family, a partner, or a legally appointed representative, someone else can often help carry their voice into the healthcare system. But many people do not have that person.
4	
5	They may live alone. They may have no children nearby. They may not have completed an advance directive. They may still be living independently, not yet eligible for full-time care, but already struggling with memory, appointments, medication, safety, and communication. In this gap between independent living and institutional care, people can become effectively unrepresented.
6	
7	When their condition worsens, doctors, social workers, and care teams are forced to make decisions with incomplete context. They may know the diagnosis, but not the person: what they value, what scares them, what routines keep them calm, what tradeoffs they would accept, or what dignity means to them. The result can be care that is medically reasonable but personally wrong.
8	
9	The core problem is not only loneliness, and it is not only care coordination. It is the gradual disappearance of the patient voice before the healthcare system has captured it.
10	
11	## Who Is Affected
12	
13	- People with early-stage dementia, Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, ALS, Huntington's, or similar progressive conditions.
14	- People living alone or without a reliable informal caregiver.
15	- People without a legally appointed representative or clear advance directive.
16	- Care teams who must make high-stakes decisions without knowing the patient's values.
17	- Municipalities, insurers, hospitals, and home-care organizations dealing with unsafe discharges, repeated crises, and fragmented support.
18	
19	## Why Now
20	
21	Ageing populations are increasing the number of people living with dementia and complex chronic illness. More older adults are living alone, while healthcare systems face staff shortages and pressure to keep people at home longer. This creates a growing group of people who are not yet in 24/7 care, but no longer fully able to advocate for themselves.
22	
23	AI voice interfaces and long-term memory systems now make it possible to capture a person's preferences gradually, conversationally, and in a way that feels more natural than forms or legal paperwork. The opportunity is to preserve the person's voice before it is lost.
24	
25	## Sharp Framing
26	
27	When memory fades, the system loses the person behind the patient.
28	
29	We need a way to capture someone's values, routines, preferences, and story while they can still express them, so that later, when they cannot speak clearly for themselves, clinicians and care teams can still understand what humane care would mean for that individual.
30	
31	## Important Boundary
32	
33	The product should not claim that AI makes medical decisions or replaces a legal representative. The stronger and safer framing is:
34	
35	The system preserves and structures the patient's voice so human decision-makers can make better, more personal, and more ethically grounded care decisions.
36

1	# GTM Netherlands/EU
2	
3	Last checked: 2026-04-25. Scope: MVP for a WhatsApp voice-note companion that preserves patient voice, builds an evidence-backed care passport, and supports later questions by HCPs/caretakers. It must not make legally or medically binding decisions.
4	
5	## Market Reality
6	
7	- Netherlands dementia burden is large and growing: Alzheimer Nederland's 2025 key figures estimate **310,000 people with dementia in 2024**, including **230,000 living at home**, **70,000 living alone**, **100,000 without a diagnosis**, and **800,000 mantelzorgers**; VWS' January 2026 strategy update expects **610,000+ people with dementia in 2050**. Sources: [Alzheimer Nederland figures PDF](https://media.alzheimer-nederland.nl/s3fs-public/media/2025-03/documents/nieuwe%20cijfers%20en%20kengetallen%20dementie%20tabellen.pdf?VersionId=ZzpCCg_AU2R7fJ14CyNWnT2F5UUmCtJQ), [VWS/Rijksoverheid NDS update](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/dementie/nieuws/2026/01/29/kabinet-maakt-nationale-dementiestrategie-toekomstbestendig).
8	- Caregiver pressure is the pain point to anchor on: the 2024 Dementiemonitor reports mantelzorgers provide **37 hours/week** on average, partners **72 hours/week**, **13%** feel very heavily burdened/overburdened, and **29%** say they can sustain care for less than a year if nothing changes. Source: [Nivel/Alzheimer Nederland Dementiemonitor 2024](https://www.nivel.nl/en/publicatie/dementiemonitor-2024-mantelzorgers-over-ondersteuning-vanuit-hun-sociale-netwerk).
9	- Timely diagnosis and proactive care planning are still gaps: among diagnosed people, **22%** of caregivers say diagnosis was not timely; proactive care planning is recommended early because verbal communication and decision capacity decline over time. Sources: [Dementiemonitor 2024](https://media.alzheimer-nederland.nl/s3fs-public/media/2024-11/documents/Dementiemonitor%202024%20-%20Landelijk%20rapport_%20def2.pdf), [Zorgstandaard Dementie - proactieve zorgplanning](https://www.zorgstandaarddementie.nl/zorgstandaard/proactieve-zorgplanning).
10	- Casemanagement is the adoption wedge: **84%** of caregivers with a home-dwelling person with dementia report a casemanager; **89%** are satisfied, but **13%** say the start was too late, with an average **16-week** wait where late. Source: [Dementiemonitor 2024](https://media.alzheimer-nederland.nl/s3fs-public/media/2024-11/documents/Dementiemonitor%202024%20-%20Landelijk%20rapport_%20def2.pdf).
11	- Digital readiness is plausible but not universal: in 2025, **93% of 65-74-year-olds** and **70% of 75+** in the Netherlands sent online text messages; WhatsApp is the largest daily Dutch social channel with **12.1M daily users**. Sources: [CBS Digitalisering 2025](https://www.cbs.nl/nl-nl/longread/rapportages/2026/digitalisering-en-kenniseconomie-2025/4-ict-gebruik-personen), [Newcom 2026](https://www.newcom.nl/nationale-sociale-media-onderzoek-2026).
12	
13	## Why Now
14	
15	- VWS updated the **Nationale Dementiestrategie 2026-2030** on 2026-01-29, reserving **EUR 23M in 2026** and more funding through 2030, with explicit emphasis on research, care/support coordination, dementia-friendly society, and the voice of people with dementia and their loved ones. Source: [Rijksoverheid](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/dementie/nieuws/2026/01/29/kabinet-maakt-nationale-dementiestrategie-toekomstbestendig).
16	- National elder-care policy is aligned with "zelf als het kan, thuis als het kan, digitaal als het kan" through WOZO and IZA. This supports a story about helping people stay known, supported, and at home longer, while reducing avoidable professional burden. Sources: [WOZO factsheet](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/ouderenzorg/documenten/publicaties/2024/12/20/wozo-zelf-als-het-kan-thuis-als-het-kan-digitaal-als-het-kan), [Rijksoverheid IZA](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/kwaliteit-van-de-zorg/integraal-zorgakkoord).
17	- Digital/hybrid care has a reimbursement vocabulary: Zorginstituut says digital/hybrid care can belong in Zvw/Wlz where it meets "stand van wetenschap en praktijk"; a technical variant of existing insured care may need less new evidence than a novel intervention. Source: [Zorginstituut handleiding digitale en hybride zorg](https://www.zorginstituutnederland.nl/documenten/2025/03/31/handleiding-beoordeling-digitale-en-hybride-zorg).
18	- Casemanagement became clearer as insured care from the **non-pluisfase** in 2024, including guidance/support, monitoring, and proactive planning. That gives the MVP a concrete workflow and buyer path. Source: [Zorginstituut casemanagement duiding](https://www.zorginstituutnederland.nl/actueel/nieuws/2024/04/23/casemanagement-dementie).
19	
20	## Stakeholders And Buyers
21	
22	- **Primary user/payer wedge:** dementia case managers and home-care organizations delivering Zvw wijkverpleging/casemanagement. Value: faster life-history capture, less repeated interviewing, better handover, structured evidence for ACP conversations.
23	- **Clinical referrers:** GP/POH, geriatrician, neurologist/memory clinic. They often initiate casemanagement: Dementiemonitor reports huisarts **41%**, geriater/neuroloog **35%**, POH **12%**. Source: [Dementiemonitor 2024](https://media.alzheimer-nederland.nl/s3fs-public/media/2024-11/documents/Dementiemonitor%202024%20-%20Landelijk%20rapport_%20def2.pdf).
24	- **Economic buyers:** contracted home-care/VVT providers, regional dementia networks, care groups, and eventually insurers/zorgkantoren if the tool proves labor-saving or improves timely support.
25	- **Municipality/social domain:** Amsterdam buurtteams/Wmo, mantelzorg support, day activities, living-at-home programs. They are not likely first payer for a health-data AI product, but they are useful pilots for prevention, loneliness, and "no family representative" pathways.
26	- **Insurers/zorgkantoren:** Zilveren Kruis is strategically relevant in Amsterdam; its Wlz V&V policy highlights self-reliance, care at home, future-proof landscape, and proven effective innovations. Source: [Zilveren Kruis Zorgkantoor V&V inkoopbeleid](https://www.zilverenkruis.nl/zorgkantoor/zorgaanbieders/zorginkoop/inkoopbeleid-vv-2024-2026).
27	- **Patient/caregiver advocates:** Alzheimer Nederland, Dementie.nl, Patiëntenfederatie, MantelzorgNL, Cliëntenbelang Amsterdam. Bring them in early for consent language and safeguarding.
28	
29	## Launch Partners / Amsterdam Hooks
30	
31	- **Amsterdam dementia routing changed recently:** as of **2025-11-03**, Amsterdam casemanagement dementia can be centrally referred via ZorgDomein/Verwijspunt 020, creating citywide insight into demand and fairer routing. Hook: "new central intake still needs a lightweight way to capture patient voice before capacity arrives." Source: [Dementie Amsterdam professionals](https://www.dementieamsterdam.nl/page/9258/professionals.html).
32	- **City policy hook:** Amsterdam reported on **2026-03-10** that it has **21 Lang Leven Thuisflats** with ~**3,600 homes**, aligned with "zorgzame buurten" and ageing in place. Source: [Gemeente Amsterdam](https://www.amsterdam.nl/nieuws/nieuwsoverzicht/woningen-ouderen/).
33	- **Care provider pilots:** Cordaan, Amsta, Amstelring, ZGAO, Buurtzorg/Evean-like home-care players. Cordaan explicitly describes casemanager dementia as reimbursed from basic insurance and not subject to own-risk contribution; ZGAO notes Amsterdam wait times can average months. Sources: [Cordaan dementia](https://www.cordaan.nl/ouderenzorg/zorg-thuis/dementie/), [ZGAO casemanager](https://www.zgao.nl/zorg-thuis/casemanager-dementie.html).
34	- **Research/ethics partners:** Alzheimercentrum Amsterdam/Amsterdam UMC, Ben Sajet Centrum, Hogeschool van Amsterdam, Vrije Universiteit, UNO Amsterdam. Alzheimercentrum is a national dementia diagnostic/research center; Ben Sajet has a specific "Diversiteit en Dementie" program and Amsterdam long-term-care network. Sources: [Alzheimercentrum Amsterdam](https://www.alzheimercentrum.nl/patienten/over-ons-centrum/), [Ben Sajet Centrum](https://bensajetcentrum.nl/programma/diversiteit-en-dementie/).
35	- **Regional coalition:** Sigra/Elaa/Gemeente Amsterdam/Zilveren Kruis/Cliëntenbelang Amsterdam "Coalitie Ouderen" focuses on vital ageing, caring neighborhoods, passende ondersteuning/zorg, divers-sensitive care, strengthening dementia chains, and Lang Leven Thuisflats. Source: [Sigra Coalitie Ouderen](https://www.sigra.nl/coalitie-ouderen).
36	- **National scaling network:** Dementie Netwerk Nederland says ~**65 regional dementia networks** are active and works on casemanagement, network organization, support in living with dementia, and information. Source: [DNN regional networks](https://dementienetwerknederland.nl/regionale-netwerken/).
37	
38	## Product Positioning
39	
40	- Position as a **voice-backed care passport assistant**, not a diagnosis, triage, treatment, or capacity-decision tool.
41	- Core workflow: consenting person sends periodic WhatsApp voice notes -> system extracts preferences, routines, values, fears, meaningful activities, social context, representative wishes -> human-readable care passport with source clips/timestamps -> HCP/case manager can ask "what did she say about food, bathing, music, medication worries, who to call, spiritual needs?" and see evidence.
42	- Start with **early-stage, consent-capable people** plus their chosen representative/casemanager. Avoid starting with non-consenting advanced dementia, surveillance, or family-only upload.
43	- Make the product valuable before any AI claims: "structured life story + ACP prompts + searchable evidence + export to care plan/PGO/ECD" is enough for a demo.
44	- For people without family/representatives, frame as "helps identify and document the person's own stated preferences early" and "supports professionals when no informal network exists," not "solves legal representation."
45	
46	## Reimbursement / Procurement Hints
47	
48	- **Zvw route:** bundle into dementia casemanagement/wijkverpleging as a digital support tool for insured care. Casemanagement includes guidance/support, monitoring, and proactive planning from the non-pluisfase when clinically indicated. Source: [Zorginstituut](https://www.zorginstituutnederland.nl/documenten/2024/04/09/duiding-vergoeding-van-casemanagement-voor-mensen-met-dementie/).
49	- **Provider procurement route:** sell pilots to VVT/home-care providers as labor-saving quality infrastructure. Buyer will ask for data processing agreement, DPIA, security, integration/export, and proof that it reduces case-manager time or improves ACP completeness.
50	- **Insurer route:** after pilot evidence, take to insurer innovation/digital-care desks or Kenniscentrum Digitale Zorg. ZN says digital care should maintain/improve quality, health, staff satisfaction, affordability, and replace rather than simply add care. Source: [ZN digitalisering](https://www.zn.nl/dossier/digitalisering/).
51	- **Wlz/zorgkantoor route:** later-stage institutional/home Wlz care, especially VPT/MPT and care at home, but procurement is slower and requires evidence of "proven effective innovations." Source: [Zilveren Kruis V&V](https://www.zilverenkruis.nl/zorgkantoor/zorgaanbieders/zorginkoop/inkoopbeleid-vv-2024-2026).
52	- **Municipal route:** Wmo/prevention subsidies can support community pilots, outreach, diversity-sensitive care, and living-at-home activities, but not the main health-data product revenue line. Amsterdam has subsidy lines for activities supporting 65+ living independently. Source: [Gemeente Amsterdam subsidy](https://www.amsterdam.nl/subsidies/subsidieregelingen/subsidie-langer-zelfstandig-wonen/).
53	- **Avoid relying on new IZA transformation money for a 24h pitch:** ZN said in 2025 new quick scans closed and many funds were already claimed; use IZA as policy alignment, not as the immediate revenue plan. Source: [ZN transformatiemiddelen update](https://www.zn.nl/actueel/nieuwe-fase-voor-transformatiegelden-integraal-zorgakkoord/).
54	
55	## Regulatory / Ethical Boundaries
56	
57	- **Consent and purpose limitation:** voice notes, transcripts, summaries, and inferences are health/sensitive personal data. Use explicit, granular consent while the person is capable; define who can access what; allow withdrawal/export; avoid secondary use unless separately consented/legal basis exists. Rijksoverheid says patients must consent to sharing medical data and access must be authorized. Source: [Rijksoverheid medical data sharing](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/digitale-gegevens-in-de-zorg/medische-gegevens-bekijken-en-delen).
58	- **Security baseline:** expect NEN 7510-aligned controls, role-based access, audit logging, MFA, data minimization, processor agreements, incident response, and a DPIA. AP says DPIA is required for likely high-risk processing; health providers must secure health records and NEN 7510 gives sector guidance. Sources: [AP DPIA](https://autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl/themas/basis-avg/praktisch-avg/data-protection-impact-assessment-dpia), [AP health data file](https://autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl/themas/gezondheid/gezondheidsgegevens-in-een-dossier).
59	- **Representation:** if a person later becomes incapacitated, Dutch practice looks first to a written directive/representative, then court-appointed mentor/curator, then spouse/partner, then family. If no one can act, the doctor may decide for medical treatment; the app should surface recorded preferences but not appoint or replace a representative. Sources: [Rijksoverheid wilsonbekwaamheid](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/levenseinde-en-euthanasie/vraag-en-antwoord/wilsonbekwaamheid), [Regelhulp wettelijke vertegenwoordiging](https://www.regelhulp.nl/onderwerpen/wettelijke-vertegenwoordiging/bewind-mentor-curatele).
60	- **Advance directives:** wilsverklaringen can record future treatment wishes and a representative; they should be made early, dated/signed, discussed with loved ones and doctors, and may not bind every medical action. Product can help draft "topics to discuss" but should not generate a legal directive as final output. Sources: [Regelhulp wilsverklaring](https://www.regelhulp.nl/onderwerpen/zorg-in-laatste-levensfase/zorgwensen-vastleggen-in-wilsverklaring), [KNMG wilsverklaring](https://www.knmg.nl/actueel/dossiers/levenseinde-2/zelfbeschikking/wilsverklaring).
61	- **Medical-device line:** if the software is intended to support diagnosis, prognosis, treatment, monitoring, or decisions that improve patient care, it may become medical device software under MDR; higher risk classes can require notified-body certification. Keep MVP intended use to documentation/search/summarization for human review. Source: [Rijksoverheid software as medical device](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/medische-hulpmiddelen/vraag-en-antwoord/wanneer-valt-software-onder-de-nieuwe-europese-regels-voor-medische-hulpmiddelen).
62	- **AI Act/EHDS:** EU AI Act is in force; high-risk AI systems, including AI-based software intended for medical purposes, require risk management, data quality, transparency, and human oversight. EHDS entered into force 2025-03-26 and will gradually require interoperable health-data access/control. Sources: [European Commission AI in healthcare](https://health.ec.europa.eu/ehealth-digital-health-and-care/artificial-intelligence-healthcare_en), [European Commission EHDS timeline](https://health.ec.europa.eu/ehealth-digital-health-and-care/european-health-data-space-regulation-ehds_en).
63	- **WhatsApp boundary:** WhatsApp is a useful low-friction capture channel, but a production medical workflow needs a formal WhatsApp Business/API data-processing model, EU hosting/transfer review, opt-in, fallback channels, and safeguards for accidental third-party voice content.
64	
65	## Defensible 24h Pitch Claims
66	
67	- "There are hundreds of thousands of people with dementia in the Netherlands, many at home and many living alone; the number is rising sharply." Defend with Alzheimer Nederland/VWS.
68	- "Case managers are highly valued but capacity and wait times are a bottleneck; our first user is the case manager, not the hospital specialist." Defend with Dementiemonitor/Zorginstituut.
69	- "The Netherlands is policy-aligned for home-first, digital-where-appropriate elder care." Defend with WOZO/IZA/NDS.
70	- "WhatsApp is a pragmatic input channel because Dutch older adults increasingly use online messaging, but we will not assume every patient can use it." Defend with CBS/Newcom.
71	- "We preserve the patient's own words and cite source snippets/timestamps for human review." This is the core trust claim.
72	- "We support ACP and care planning conversations; we do not make medical, legal, euthanasia, capacity, or placement decisions."
73	
74	## Claims To Avoid
75	
76	- "We can decide what the patient would have wanted."
77	- "This replaces a legal representative, mentor, curator, notary, physician, or casemanager."
78	- "This is an advance directive/wilsverklaring."
79	- "This is diagnostic/prognostic/treatment decision support."
80	- "WhatsApp is automatically compliant for medical records."
81	- "Insurers will reimburse this immediately as a standalone app."
82	- "AI solves dementia care worker shortages." Safer: "AI can reduce repeated information gathering and help professionals spend scarce time on human care."
83	
84	## Likely Objections And Counters
85	
86	- **"Patients may not be capable of valid consent."** Start before capacity declines; record consent status and chosen access roles; support re-consent/check-ins; stop collection when capacity/consent is unclear unless a lawful representative and provider workflow are in place.
87	- **"Hallucinations could harm care."** No free-floating answers: every answer must cite source notes/clips, show confidence and gaps, and route uncertainty to "ask the person/representative/casemanager."
88	- **"This creates more data and liability."** Keep a bounded care-passport schema, deletion/retention rules, audit log, and human sign-off before anything enters the formal care record.
89	- **"Care teams already have ECD/PGO systems."** Do not compete with ECD; export structured summaries and source references. Product is pre-documentation capture and retrieval of the patient's own voice.
90	- **"Families could manipulate the record."** Separate patient-recorded statements from third-party notes; label provenance; require human review; allow contested/updated statements.
91	- **"Digital exclusion."** Offer assisted recording with case manager/volunteer, phone-call capture, and non-digital paper interview fallback. WhatsApp is an entry point, not the only path.
92	- **"Medical device/GDPR risk."** Deliberately constrain intended use to documentation and human-reviewed care planning support; run DPIA; use NEN 7510-aligned controls; get medtech/legal review before adding recommendations.
93	
94	## MVP Pilot Shape
95	
96	- 20-30 early-stage participants through one Amsterdam provider/network; include a "no close family" subgroup if ethically approvable.
97	- 8-week capture period: weekly WhatsApp voice prompt + optional spontaneous notes.
98	- Case-manager dashboard: care passport sections, unanswered prompts, source-backed Q&A, export PDF/structured JSON.
99	- Success metrics: case-manager minutes saved on life-history intake, ACP topic completeness, number of patient-authored preferences captured, caregiver/casemanager trust score, zero unsupported answer rate in QA review.
100	- Ethics/safety: consent script, representative/access register, DPIA checklist, clinical escalation rule for risk statements, deletion/export process.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the "This is Me" document from Alzheimer's Society:

"This is me" is a simple leaflet for anyone receiving professional care who is living with dementia or experiencing delirium or other communication difficulties.

"This is me" can be used to record details about a person who can't easily share information about themselves, and it helps health and social care professionals better understand who the person really is, which can help them deliver care that is tailored to the person's needs.

It can help reduce distress for people with dementia and their carers, and can also help overcome problems with communication and prevent more serious conditions such as malnutrition and dehydration.

The leaflet can be used in any care setting – at home, in hospital, in respite care or in a care home.

Now in its fourth edition, "This is me" has been endorsed by the Royal College of Nursing since 2010, and the latest edition includes changes that were suggested by people living with dementia, carers and health and social care professionals.

The document is available on the Alzheimer's Society website at www.alzheimers.org.uk/thisisme.

"Praat op Tijd" (which translates to "Talk on Time") is an Advance Care Planning program for healthcare providers of people with intellectual disabilities in palliative care. The program provides various practical tools to support caregivers in timely discussions about the wishes and preferences of clients and their families.

The program is explained on the ACP website of Koraal, where various handy documents can be downloaded.

This tool is specifically designed for use with vulnerable populations, particularly people with intellectual disabilities who may need support in advance care planning discussions. An Advance Care Planning (or Proactive Care Planning) conversation typically involves a discussion with a doctor, client, family or legal representative and other important people including professional caregivers, where future situations are discussed and the best course of action is determined. Usually multiple conversations are needed and the topic comes up on the agenda again over time.

## Overview

StoryCorps launched the Memory Loss Initiative to support and encourage people with various forms of memory loss to share their stories with loved ones and future generations. These interviews help people with Alzheimer's disease and other forms of memory loss get their stories on tape.

## Collection and Archive

StoryCorps has collected more than 1,800 of these interviews. All of the interviews are archived in the Library of Congress and are now part of the nation's oral history.

## Purpose and Impact

The StoryCorps experience was a meaningful activity that allowed participants to acknowledge the beauty of the present moment, to reflect and engage in meaningful conversations, to re-affirm both the selfhood of individuals with memory loss and their relationships with family members, and to be a part of national history while leaving a legacy for future generations within families.

## Tools and Resources

StoryCorps has created a unique toolkit, Commemorate, a free resource to organizations to enable them to record, share, and preserve the stories of clients living with memory loss. StoryCorps has a specific DIY course designed for organizations serving clients living with memory loss, offering insights and best practices for creating a recording program to honor and preserve the stories of your clients.

## Advance Care Planning in the Netherlands

Advance Care Planning (ACP), also known as proactieve zorgplanning, supports healthcare providers in executing ACP (proactive care planning). The Leidraad proactieve zorgplanning (Guideline for Proactive Care Planning) was developed by organizations including NHG, Verenso, NVAVG, V&VN, and Patiëntenfederatie Nederland (Patient Federation Netherlands).

## Digital Documentation

A newly developed digital fillable format can be used for uniform documentation and recognizable transfer of established treatment wishes and limitations. This addresses an important gap, as the lack of digital documentation and shared systems across settings has been identified as a barrier to ACP implementation.

## Patiëntenfederatie Nederland's Role

Patiëntenfederatie Nederland is among the organizations involved in developing guidelines and working groups for proactive care planning.

## Research Developments

## MyDirectives Overview

MyDirectives offers individuals a simple, secure, easy to use platform for creating, uploading, and storing advance care planning documents at no cost. MyDirectives solutions make advance care planning simple, provide secure cloud storage and enable all stakeholders to have access to the ACP documents they need, whenever and wherever they need it.

## Key Features

Users can upload existing ACP documents, advance directives, mental health advance directives, portable medical order (PMO) forms (POLST), and even videos, allowing users to keep all their important healthcare information in one secure place. Healthcare preferences are securely stored in the cloud, giving users peace of mind that their information is always accessible when they or their families and healthcare providers need it.

## Technology & Security

MyDirectives offers the only digital ACP tools and interoperable cloud-based storage that is HITRUST Risk-Based, 2-Year Certified. The A|D Vault Exchange™ registry and repository is a secure, cloud-based storage solution that ensures accessibility anytime, anywhere.

## Universal Advance Digital Directive (uADD)

MyDirectives® has an "Universal Advance Digital Directive (uADD)TM", which is a medical power of attorney/living will/autopsy preference form created by the company that the company states is a "legal document" that "should be enforceable" in all states. However, the site does not address what is required in each state, and the Terms and Conditions state that users are responsible for complying with the laws of their particular state.

## What is a PGO?

A PGO stands for Persoonlijke GezondheidsOmgeving (Personal Health Environment) and is a secure online place where you can find all information about your health. The benefit of a PGO is that it brings together all your data from different healthcare organizations in one place, so you only need to log in once and remember one password, and because the PGO is directly linked to, for example, your hospital, GP, and pharmacy, the information you see is always up-to-date.

## Quli and Ivido

Quli is a Persoonlijke GezondheidsOmgeving where you have all your healthcare data organized in one place. With Ivido, when you create an account, you get free access to your Persoonlijke Gezondheidsomgeving, which is initially empty but where you can add your own data and retrieve data from healthcare organizations.

Both Quli and Ivido are recommended PGOs and are connected to MedMij, which ensures secure transfer of medical information.

## Dementia and Caregivers

Some patients are not able to make decisions about their care themselves, for example people with physical or mental limitations or dementia. Caregivers must then make decisions for them. It is very important that they know exactly what is medically happening with this person. With a PGO, the caregiver can view all health information and communicate with healthcare providers.

## PinkRoccade ECD and Home Care Solutions

PinkRoccade Care offers mijnCaress ECD and DZP for records, workflows and collaboration in nursing, home and disability care. PinkRoccade Care delivers mijnCaress, an electronic client record and care management platform for nursing homes, home care and disability care providers.

The company is based in Apeldoorn, Gelderland, The Netherlands. Services include implementation, customer care, IT support and consultancy for care-process digitization.

## Dementia Case Management in the Netherlands

The search results also reveal broader context about dementia care management in the Netherlands. Dementia care in the Netherlands is shifting from fragmented, ad hoc care to more coordinated and personalised care, with case management contributing to this shift. 

Dutch policy stipulates that people with dementia should remain at home for as long as possible, preferably appealing to family, friends and neighbours, with professional help and nursing homes deemed last resorts. Case managers play a pivotal role in helping people with dementia and their informal caretakers remain at home for as long as possible.

The search results indicate that case management is a strongly promoted intervention in the Netherlands, and dementia case managers are becoming a well-established part of the care for people with dementia.

Here are the search results for "Storii life story phone call seniors dementia reminiscence":

## Overview

Storii is a story-telling platform that allows people to record their life stories with no computer, smartphone or internet required, instead recording memories as people relay them over any phone, including landlines.

## How It Works

Storii allows people to document their life stories simply by answering questions about their lives in a series of phone calls over a period of a year. Storii can be set to automatically call your loved one at any phone number up to three times a week at days/times that are convenient, throughout the one-year subscription period. Storii's AI Biography Rewrites feature helps generate a well-crafted narrative by analyzing the entire recording to clarify, organize or add additional context to enhance each story.

## Benefits for Seniors and Dementia Care

Storii offers benefits including combating isolation through connection, providing users with a sense of purpose and helping improve psychological well-being with reminiscence therapy. For older adults, especially those with dementia, reminiscing taps into long-term memory, which often remains intact even as short-term recall fades, providing cognitive stimulation and comfort.

Life story work brings about a greater understanding of a person's needs and wishes and can be used to support reminiscence therapy as their condition progresses. It can improve care outcomes by giving care providers a deep understanding of the individual's background, personality, interests and needs.

## Familienet

Familienet is a communication app for healthcare. Users can easily share messages, photos and videos with family and volunteers, and with the Meehelp-request feature, you can directly arrange help with activities.

On Familienet, a client of a care facility gets their own page with a photo, which is filled with messages and information about that client, including photos of activities or events. The page is password-protected.

Familienet was founded by two brothers who noticed they were missing information about their mother who was in a care facility.

The app appears to be widely used in Dutch elderly care settings to facilitate better communication between care workers, family members (mantelzorgers), and care recipients.

## Key Information

Reminiscing involves retrieving old memories, which is a valuable approach for people with dementia in the Netherlands. 

### Life Stories and Memory Books

Memories can be documented together with photos and images from the past in a life book or life album, which becomes a powerful tool especially as dementia progresses. By viewing the photos in the life book and reading the short texts together, personal contact can be made with someone with dementia.

### Support and Resources

Those wanting to create a life book can find instructions on the website Zorginzicht.nl from the Dutch Health Institute, and there are many local volunteer initiatives to help with creating a life book. Humanitas has life book projects in several locations, and you can check on their website www.humanitas.nl if this is available in your area.

### Benefits of Reminiscing

Remembering still relevant information builds confidence and increases self-esteem, and it is the only way for people with dementia to preserve their identity.

## Lifelogging and AI Memory in 2025

In 2025, the era of Perfect Recall has arrived, with tools that search your life rather than just the web. The major players in this space are:

**Limitless (formerly Rewind)**: Limitless began life as Rewind, a software tool aimed at capturing digital activity so users could "search their memory" later. Limitless started as a software-first lifelogging product that captured desktop activity and turned it into a searchable memory, then pivoted into hardware by launching a small AI pendant that records in-person conversations for later transcripts and summaries.

**Meta Acquisition**: By 2025, Limitless AI had captured significant attention in the AI wearables market, leading to its acquisition by Meta in December of that year. With the acquisition complete, Limitless will stop selling its hardware devices and begin winding down parts of its existing software lineup, with hardware sales immediately ceasing.

## Eldercare Applications

The search results include an interesting eldercare use case: A retired individual with good health started to forget fine points in conversations at doctor appointments or items needed at the grocery store, and the app creates text transcripts of conversations that can be searched to find specific details.

## Important Update

Alexa Together is no longer available as of May 21, 2025, and has been replaced by Alexa Emergency Assist for remote caregiver service. Additionally, Amazon discontinued Alexa Together in June of 2024.

## Previous Alexa Together Features (Historical)

Before its discontinuation, Alexa Together offered several caregiver and elderly support features:

**Emergency Response & Safety:**
- A 24/7 emergency helpline allowing seniors to request emergency services by saying "Alexa, call for help," with caregivers also being notified when the emergency helpline is contacted.
- Compatibility with third-party fall detection devices from Assistive Technology Service and Vayyar.

**Caregiver Connectivity:**
- Circle of Support enabling up to 10 caregivers to connect to a device and receive daily alerts.
- Remote Assist function allowing caregivers to set reminders, add contacts, manage grocery lists, and adjust device settings.
- Activity feed that collected all Alexa Together features in the Alexa app for caregivers to manage alerts and access features.

**Pricing:**
- The service cost $19.99 per month or $199 per year.

For current remote caregiver services with Alexa, you would need to look into the newer Alexa Emergency Assist option.

## Overview

ACP Decisions was founded in 2010 by Drs. Angelo Volandes and Aretha Delight Davis with the purpose of empowering patients and their families to make informed medical decisions through evidence-based video media.

## Services and Resources

The ACP Decisions library has expanded to 500+ patient/caregiver facing videos in 25 languages that cover a range of clinical topics. The My ACP Decisions platform supports clinicians, patients, and families during their shared advance care planning journey.

## Leadership

Dr. Volandes is a physician researcher, Professor of Medicine at the Geisel School of Medicine at Dartmouth, Vice Chair of Research at Dartmouth Health, and an internationally recognized expert on the use of video decision support tools, decision science, and ethics who leads an internationally recognized group of innovators and video artists who create video support tools to better inform patients about their options for medical care.

Dr. Davis is a co-Founder and CEO of the Foundation with dual training in law and medicine, and leads efforts to empower thousands of patients and families across the country with culturally attuned, evidence-based video tools that support informed, values-based medical decisions.

## Research Impact

The ACP Decisions videos have been used in numerous clinical trials across different patient populations, including studies with advanced cancer patients, heart failure patients, nursing home residents with dementia, and adolescents with advanced cancer, demonstrating their widespread use in advancing advance care planning discussions.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about care passports and dementia digital tools in the UK NHS:

## Health and Care Passports

The health and care passport is designed to be a quick and easy way to give health and social care professionals more information about you to help them provide right care and treatment and understand what reasonable adjustments they should make. The health and care passport can be used for planned appointments and in an emergency.

## Dementia-Specific Solutions

The Public Health Agency, in association with the Royal College of General Practitioners and voluntary sector organisations, have developed a Healthcare Passport which will help carry significant aspects of the individual's life and provide meaningful information between people living with dementia and health and social care services.

Additionally, the MiCode digital passport is a personalised QR code that allows for instant access to vital health information, reducing delays for emergency services, healthcare professionals, the police, and families, by providing immediate clarity when a person with dementia is confused or lost.

## NHS App Integration

The NHS App is becoming the "digital front door" to services, and in the future, patients will be able to manage all their appointments, test results, and care plans through one place, with carers and partners able to access the app on their behalf.

The search results show various digital passport and care solutions being developed or piloted across the UK health system for dementia care, though there isn't a single unified "care passport" app mentioned specifically for 2025.

Based on the search results, here's information about Cake and end-of-life planning:

## Overview

Cake is a free digital tool that helps you proactively discover, store, and share your end-of-life preferences with loved ones. Foundation Partners Group has made the strategic decision to acquire Cake, and since its launch, over 100,000,000 people have used Cake's extensive catalog of more than 5,000 articles and planning tools to navigate the intricacies of end-of-life planning including wills, funeral arrangements, advance care directives, estate settlement and grief.

## Advance Directive Support

Cake allows you to create your will, advance directive, and power of attorney from the comfort of home. Additionally, Cake hosts a free, comprehensive resource where visitors can download advance directives by US state. For healthcare organizations, the tool can drive completion of advance directives and other key end-of-life planning information and make the information easily accessible to care teams, family, and friends so that end of life preferences can be honored.

## Features

Based on user answers, the app creates a profile for each user that is divided into four categories: legacy, health, legal/financial, and funeral, with each category accompanied by action steps one could take to carry out those wishes.

## Security & Membership

Cake is HIPAA compliant, employs bank-level security, and all of your data is protected in transit and at rest with 256-bit AES encryption and 2048-bit certificates. Premium membership is currently available for $24 per year and its added features include access to a wider range of discounted services—such as will-making, funeral services and planning—as well as access to Cake's team of live experts who provide guidance and emotional support to members.

  "content": "# Competitor Landscape — Voice-Backed Care Passport

Last checked: 2026-04-25

Scope: who else is going after "capture the patient's voice early so professionals can deliver more personal dementia care later" — and how we should position. Skips obvious non-fits; flags overlap, defensibility, and gaps.

## A. Voice-capture / life-story / digital-legacy products

### Memoirji — https://memoirji.com/
- **What:** WhatsApp-based AI biographer ("Patience") that interviews users via voice notes and produces a structured PDF memoir, free.
- **Geography / stage:** Indie consumer, global. Positioned in 2026 round-ups as the leading WhatsApp-native life-story tool ([Memoirji blog](https://memoirji.com/blog/best-ai-memoir-tools-2025/)).
- **Overlap (axis 1):** High on input channel — same WhatsApp + voice-note wedge we plan to use for capture. They explicitly target elderly parents.
- **Why we still win:** Memoirji's output is a memoir for the family bookshelf. We produce a structured, source-cited care passport with provenance, ACP topic coverage, and a professional-facing query layer. They have no case-manager workflow, no clinical schema, no DPIA / NEN 7510 story, no Dutch/EU hosting commitment. The capture UX is a commodity; the retrieval/evidence layer is the moat.
- **Risk:** They could pivot toward eldercare retrieval. Pre-empt by anchoring on case-manager workflow + Dutch reimbursement.

### Storii — https://www.storii.com/
- **What:** Phone-call (landline OK) life-story interviewer, weekly questions, AI biography rewrites, paid annual subscription.
- **Geography / stage:** UK/US, early-commercial; explicit dementia/reminiscence positioning ([AgeInPlaceTech](https://www.ageinplacetech.com/pressrelease/storii-creates-persons-life-memoir-over-phone)).
- **Overlap (axis 1):** High on capture, medium on dementia framing. Sister product StoriiCare is a care-home activities platform, not a clinical preference store.
- **Why we still win:** Same gap — no structured ACP/preferences schema, no integration with Dutch case-management workflow, no source-cited Q&A for HCPs. They optimise for reminiscence (good feeling), we optimise for evidence-backed retrieval (good decisions).

### StoryCorps Memory Loss Initiative — https://storycorps.org/discover/memory-loss-initiative/
- **What:** Non-profit oral-history program; 1,800+ recorded interviews archived at the Library of Congress; "Commemorate" toolkit for organisations.
- **Geography / stage:** US non-profit, mature, philanthropic.
- **Overlap (axis 1):** Conceptual overlap (preserving voice in dementia) but not productised for care workflows.
- **Why we still win:** They are an archive, not a tool. Useful as evidence the *idea* of preserving the patient voice has academic and cultural legitimacy ([Savundranayagam et al. 2011, *Dementia*](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/1471301211408123)). Cite as validation, not as a rival.

### Tell Mel, Life Story AI, StoriedLife AI, HereAfter, Memory Lane, Eternos
- **What:** AI phone/voice biographers ("interactive memory app", "voice clone after death"). Eternos raised $10.3M in Nov 2025 ([TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2025/11/11/immortality-startup-eternos-pivots-to-a-personal-ai-that-sounds-like-you/)).
- **Overlap (axis 1):** Crowded consumer voice-memoir layer.
- **Why we still win:** None target healthcare buyers, ACP, or case managers; their wedge is gifting/legacy. The category proves capture works at scale; nobody has built the professional retrieval surface.

## B. Advance Care Planning (ACP) tools

### Praat op Tijd / Leidraad Proactieve Zorgplanning — Palliaweb / Patiëntenfederatie
- **What:** Dutch national ACP guidance + a newly-developed digital fillable form for uniform documentation of treatment wishes, co-developed by NHG, Verenso, NVAVG, V&VN, Patiëntenfederatie ([Palliaweb leidraad](https://palliaweb.nl/proactieve-zorgplanning), [zorgwensenvoorlater.nu PDF](https://zorgwensenvoorlater.nu/storage/Leidraad-proactieve-zorgplanning.pdf)). "Praat op Tijd" itself is Koraal's ACP toolkit for people with intellectual disabilities ([Koraal](https://www.koraal.nl/gehandicaptenzorg/wat-maakt-ons-bijzonder/zorg-dichtbij/advance-care-planning)).
- **Overlap (axis 2):** High — same regulator/professional ecosystem we must integrate with.
- **Why we still win / where we don't:** This is the *standard*, not a competitor — it's a form/leidraad, not a capture tool. We should explicitly export to its schema. Risk: if VWS/Patiëntenfederatie later commission a national ACP capture app, that becomes our reference platform. Build to plug in, not displace.

### MyDirectives — https://www.mydirectives.com/
- **What:** US digital advance-directive platform, HITRUST-certified, cloud-stored uADD with provider exchange.
- **Geography / stage:** US, mature, integrates with EHRs.
- **Overlap (axis 2):** Medium — outputs are legal directives; ours are not.
- **Why we still win:** They assume the user can fill structured forms today. Our wedge is *before* form-filling capacity exists, capturing values/routines/fears in the patient's own words. Dutch wilsverklaring is also a legal artefact KNMG warns shouldn't be auto-generated ([KNMG](https://www.knmg.nl/actueel/dossiers/levenseinde-2/zelfbeschikking/wilsverklaring)) — we should not produce legally binding output.

### Cake (now "Altogether") — https://www.joincake.com/
- **What:** Consumer end-of-life planning platform; wills, advance directives, funeral. Acquired by Foundation Partners Group in 2024.
- **Overlap (axis 2):** Low for our wedge — they target healthy adults planning ahead, sold via funeral-home channel.
- **Why we still win:** Different buyer (consumer/funeral vs. case manager); different artefact (legal docs vs. searchable voice evidence).

### ACP Decisions — https://www.acpdecisions.org/
- **What:** Dr. Angelo Volandes' video-decision-aid library, 500+ videos, 25 languages, RCT-validated for nursing-home dementia ACP ([JAMA Network 2018](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29868778/)).
- **Overlap (axis 2):** Adjacent — they educate the patient *into* an ACP conversation; we capture what comes out of one (and what comes out of life in general).
- **Why we still win:** Complementary, not competing. Pitch as "ACP Decisions makes the conversation possible; we make the patient's voice durable across years."

## C. Care passport / dementia preference platforms

### "This is me" — Alzheimer's Society UK — https://www.alzheimers.org.uk/get-support/publications-factsheets/this-is-me
- **What:** Free paper leaflet (4th edition), RCN-endorsed since 2010, captures who-the-person-is for hospital/care-home staff.
- **Overlap (axis 3):** Highest conceptual overlap of any product. This is our category-defining incumbent.
- **Why we still win:** It is paper, one-shot, completed by family/staff (not the patient), and not searchable. Evidence shows uptake and fidelity in hospitals are mixed ([Baillie & Thomas 2020, *Dementia*](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/1471301218778907); [PubMed integrative review](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33555640/)). We are "This is Me, voice-native, longitudinal, queryable, with provenance." Pitch frame: we should *literally name-check* This is Me as the inspiration and position as the digital, patient-authored evolution.

### NHS Health & Care Passport / MiCode QR passport
- **What:** NHS England plain-English health-and-care passport ([NHS England](https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/health-and-care-passport-plain-english/)); MiCode QR-code passport piloting in UK dementia.
- **Overlap (axis 3):** Medium — UK-only, form-driven, no voice capture.
- **Why we still win:** Different country, different artefact. Useful precedent that "passport" framing lands with health systems.

### Herinneringen / levensboek tradition (NL)
- **What:** Dutch "levensboek" practice, supported by Humanitas volunteers, Vilans/Zorginzicht templates, Innovatiekring Dementie ([IDé](https://www.innovatiekringdementie.nl/a-1931/levensverhalen-en-dierbare-herinneringen-vastleggen/)).
- **Overlap (axis 3):** Cultural — every Dutch case manager knows what a levensboek is.
- **Why we still win:** Levensboek is paper/photo, family-driven, not searchable, not exported to ECD. Pitch: "digital, voice-first levensboek with care-passport export."

## D. Dutch case-management / ECD systems

### Nedap Ons — https://nedap-ons.nl/
- **What:** Dominant Dutch ECD for V&V/GHZ/GGZ. 1,900+ institutions, 400k+ professionals; 67 of 115 examined elderly-care institutions use it ([Consultancy.nl](https://www.consultancy.nl/nieuws/55950/nedap-de-grootste-ecd-leverancier-binnen-ouderenzorg-en-gehandicaptenzorg)). Has Caren as the family-facing portal ([caren.nl](https://caren.nl/)).
- **Overlap (axis 4 + 7):** They own the system of record. They could ship a voice-capture module — but their roadmap signals are capacity planning and Luna (long-stay-at-home), not preference capture.
- **Why we still win:** Building an ECD is not our game. Build *to* Ons via export/integration; sell to providers as "the missing pre-documentation layer that fills Caren and Ons with the patient's actual words." Caren is the obvious distribution surface for the patient/family side.

### PinkRoccade Healthcare — mijnCaress — https://www.pinkroccade-healthcare.nl/
- **What:** #2 Dutch ECD for V&V/home-care/GHZ. Same logic as Nedap.
- **Overlap (axis 4):** Same — integration target, not a competitor unless they ship voice capture.

### Cordaan / Amstelring / Buurtzorg in-house tools
- **Overlap:** Low. These are buyers, not builders. Worth confirming that none of the 65 regional dementia networks ([DNN](https://dementienetwerknederland.nl/regionale-netwerken/)) has commissioned a voice-capture pilot.

## E. AI companions / monitoring for older adults

### ElliQ (Intuition Robotics) — https://elliq.com/
- **What:** Tabletop AI companion robot; daily check-ins, wellness, video calls, reminders. NY State Office for the Aging deployed ~800 units, reported 95% loneliness reduction ([NY OFA](https://aging.ny.gov/elliq-proactive-care-companion-initiative)). 2025 launch of ElliQ Caregiver Solution ([Robot Report](https://www.therobotreport.com/elliq-caregiver-solution-intuition-robotics-remote-home-care/)).
- **Overlap (axis 5):** Medium — they capture conversations, target older adults, expanding to caregivers.
- **Why we still win:** ElliQ is a $1500 device + subscription, English-only, not Dutch-deployed, optimised for loneliness/engagement not preference extraction. We are software-first on a channel (WhatsApp) seniors already use. They could add a "care passport" feature — possible but unlikely first-order roadmap; they're chasing Medicare/state wellness contracts, not Dutch case management.

### Sensi.AI — https://www.sensi.ai/
- **What:** Audio-based "Care Intelligence" — passive ambient listening in the home, detects falls/distress/early dementia signals. $45M Series C Oct 2025 ([Calcalist](https://www.calcalistech.com/ctechnews/article/hyawomspex)). Used by 80% of major US home-care networks.
- **Overlap (axis 5):** Lower than it looks — they are *passive* audio surveillance for safety, we are *consented active* voice capture for preference documentation. Different consent model, different output, different buyer pain.
- **Why we still win:** Sensi is a clinical-signals product (analogous to remote patient monitoring); we are a documentation-and-retrieval product. They could conceivably extend to "what does she usually say about food?" but it's not their pitch and the always-on mic privacy posture is much harder in NL/EU under AVG/AI Act.

### Replika-for-elders, Embodied (Moxie shutdown 2024), Inspiren
- **Overlap:** Mostly companionship/clinical-monitoring. Not directly competing for the case-manager workflow.

## F. Caregiver-coordination apps

### Familienet (NL) — https://www.familienet.nl/
- **What:** Family/care-team messaging app for Dutch elderly-care facilities; photos, updates, "Meehelp" tasks.
- **Overlap (axis 6):** Low on preference capture, high on social-distribution model.
- **Why we still win:** They are a comms layer, not a knowledge layer. Possible distribution partner for family-facing onboarding.

### Caren (Nedap) — https://caren.nl/
- **What:** Family/client portal tied to Nedap Ons; family can view/contribute to the care file.
- **Overlap (axes 6 + 7):** Medium. The natural place for our care passport to surface to the family.
- **Why we still win:** Caren shows formal care-record content to family; it does not capture the patient's spoken voice over time. Integration target.

### CaringBridge / Lotsa Helping Hands / Carely
- **Overlap:** US/coordination-focused; not players in the Dutch case-management workflow.

## G. PGOs / health-record adjacents (NL)

### Quli, Ivido, other MedMij-certified PGOs
- **What:** Dutch personal health environments aggregating data from GP/hospital/pharmacy via MedMij ([Rijksoverheid PGO](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/digitale-gegevens-in-de-zorg/vraag-en-antwoord/waar-kan-ik-een-persoonlijke-gezondheidsomgeving-pgo-voor-gebruiken)).
- **Overlap (axis 7):** Adjacent. PGOs are records of clinical *facts*, not patient *voice*. Quli notes caregivers can act on behalf of someone with dementia.
- **Why we still win:** PGOs do not capture preferences/values; they aggregate forms. Eventually we may export to a PGO, but the wedge is the case-manager dashboard, not consumer PGO adoption (which has been famously slow).

## H. Generalist AI-memory plays

### Limitless (acquired by Meta, Dec 2025), Personal.ai, Rewind (sunset)
- **What:** Lifelogging, ambient transcription. Limitless's pendant-and-software lifelog was bought by Meta and hardware sales killed in Dec 2025 ([WinBuzzer](https://winbuzzer.com/2025/12/05/meta-acquires-ai-wearables-startup-limitless-kills-pendant-sales-and-sunsets-rewind-app-xcxwbn/)).
- **Overlap (axis 8):** Low for our wedge. Different buyer, different consent model, no clinical positioning.
- **Why we still win:** Generalist memory plays are pivoting into Meta-scale platform features. They will not own the dementia case-manager workflow — but if Meta ships "WhatsApp Memory" inside WhatsApp itself, that changes the channel story. Worth tracking.

## I. Big-tech / platforms

### Apple Health/Journal, Google Health voice memos, Microsoft Healthcare Bot
- **Overlap:** Latent. None has shipped a "patient voice for case managers" product. Apple Journal added voice notes; Google has discontinued multiple consumer-health bets; Microsoft Healthcare Bot is task automation.
- **Amazon Alexa Together** was discontinued in 2024 and replaced with the narrower "Alexa Emergency Assist" in May 2025 ([SafeWise](https://www.safewise.com/what-is-alexa-together/)). Signals that big-tech caregiver pivots have not been profitable consumer plays.
- **Why we still win:** Big tech is a channel risk (Meta owns WhatsApp), not a product competitor today. Mitigation: design for channel-agnostic capture (WhatsApp + phone-call fallback + assisted in-person).

## J. Academic / public-sector NL

- **Vilans** — runs "DEMTECH" and tech-at-home dementia work, surveys mantelzorgers using AI chatbots ([Vilans](https://www.vilans.nl/actueel/verhalen/ai-chatbots-zelfredzaamheid-zorg)). Knowledge institute, not a vendor — possible research/validation partner.
- **Alzheimercentrum Amsterdam / Amsterdam UMC** — TAP-dementia diagnosis project, no voice-capture product.
- **Ben Sajet Centrum, HvA, VU, UNO Amsterdam** — research/ethics partners, not competitors.
- **Risk:** A ZonMw-funded consortium could commission something similar. Get in early as a research partner, not a target.

---

## Strategic takeaways

1. **The capture layer is crowded; the retrieval-for-professionals layer is empty.** A dozen consumer AI biographers (Memoirji, Storii, Tell Mel, HereAfter, Eternos) already do voice-note life-story capture. Nobody owns the dementia case-manager dashboard with source-cited Q&A and ACP-schema export. That is our wedge — not "we built a WhatsApp voice bot."
2. **Position as the digital, voice-native evolution of "This is Me" and the Dutch levensboek — not a new category.** Both have professional credibility (RCN endorsement; Vilans/Zorginzicht templates; Alzheimer Nederland reach) and known limitations (paper, one-shot, not searchable, not patient-authored). Anchoring there is more defensible than "AI memory for elders."
3. **ACP tools assume capacity at the moment of use; we capture *before* capacity declines.** MyDirectives, Cake, and the Dutch Leidraad all need a coherent author today. Our pitch is the upstream feeder: accumulate the patient's own voice on routines/values/fears so that when the formal ACP conversation happens, the case manager arrives with evidence, not a blank form.
4. **The ECD vendors (Nedap Ons, PinkRoccade) are integration targets, not competitors — but only if we move first.** Their roadmaps are capacity, billing, and Caren-style family portals. A voice-capture module is plausible-but-not-imminent. Build the export/integration story now (Ons API, Caren surfacing) so we are the obvious bolt-on before they build it.
5. **Privacy/consent is the moat against ambient-audio plays (Sensi.AI) and big-tech channel plays (Meta/WhatsApp).** Active consented capture, NEN 7510-aligned controls, DPIA done, EU hosting, source-cited (no hallucinated synthesis), human-in-the-loop sign-off — these are explicitly things Sensi's always-on-mic model and a hypothetical "WhatsApp Memory" feature cannot easily promise under AVG/AI Act / EHDS. Make this part of the pitch, not the appendix.

## Follow-up questions to investigate next

1. **Does Nedap Ons (and PinkRoccade mijnCaress) have a documented external API or app-marketplace for a third-party voice-capture module to plug into the case-manager view and Caren?** Would determine whether our "integration not displacement" story is technically real in 8 weeks.
2. **Has Patiëntenfederatie / NHG / Verenso commissioned (or signalled intent to commission) a national digital ACP capture tool around the new Leidraad?** If yes, our path is "reference implementation / capture front-end for the Leidraad" rather than parallel product.
3. **What is the formal data-processor / EU-hosting status of WhatsApp Business Cloud API for Dutch health data in 2026?** Currently the lowest-friction capture channel; if AP/Zorginstituut have published anything restrictive, we need a phone-call fallback as primary, not secondary.

## Things we should NOT claim in the pitch

- "First product to preserve the patient's voice in dementia." StoryCorps Memory Loss Initiative (since 2010) and 1,800+ Library-of-Congress recordings predate us.
- "First WhatsApp life-story bot." Memoirji is shipping now and is positioned as the leading WhatsApp memoir tool.
- "First AI care companion for elders." ElliQ is deployed at state scale; Sensi is in 80% of major US home-care networks; the category is funded.
- "Replaces the levensboek / This is Me / wilsverklaring / case manager." We extend, structure, and digitise — we do not replace any of these. KNMG, Rijksoverheid, and the Leidraad are explicit about boundaries.
- "Detects dementia / cognitive decline from voice." That is medical-device territory under MDR + AI Act. Sensi.AI walks that line; we should not.
- "Reimbursed by Zvw today." Reimbursement is a credible 12-24 month path via wijkverpleging/casemanagement, not a current claim.

1	# Distribution: Adapter + Standalone UI
2	
3	**Decision date:** 2026-04-25
4	
5	## The strategy
6	
7	We are not a new patient-portal vendor. We are not a new ECD. We are an **engine that captures the patient's voice and structures it**, and we ship in two modes:
8	
9	1. **Adapter mode (primary)** — write the structured care-passport output into the patient/family-facing "voice" slots that already exist in dominant Dutch ECDs and portals (Nedap Caren's *Mijn verhaal*, PinkRoccade's equivalent, future MedMij PGO life-story fields). We do not replace those slots; we fill them with structured, voice-native, source-cited content the slot was always meant to hold.
10	2. **Standalone UI (secondary)** — a small in-house web view for cases where the adapter target doesn't exist or isn't reachable: hospital admission, locum nurse, organisations on niche ECDs, family carers without a portal account, or pre-pilot demos. The standalone view shows the same care passport with the same provenance, gated by patient/representative consent.
11	
12	Why this is the right shape:
13	- **Distribution.** Caregivers and case managers already log in to *something* daily. We meet them there instead of asking for one more login.
14	- **Trust.** "We feed the field your ECD already has" is easier to explain to a procurement officer than "adopt our new system."
15	- **Defensibility.** The valuable output is the structured-and-cited care passport, not the rendering surface. We can change rendering surfaces; competitors can't easily change what we capture.
16	- **Pitch clarity.** "We are the engine, not the field" gives us a one-liner against Nedap if/when they extend *Mijn verhaal* themselves.
17	
18	## Integration surfaces (what we know, what we need)
19	
20	### Known with reasonable confidence
21	
22	| Surface | Vendor | Slot | Evidence | Adapter readiness |
23	|---|---|---|---|---|
24	| **Caren / Ons Dossier** | Nedap | *Mijn verhaal* / *Persoonlijk cliëntverhaal* | Shipped feature, free-text + photo, family-authored, editing temporarily disabled at time of writing | API status unknown — likely user-facing only; needs investigation |
25	| **mijnCaress / Ecare / Pluriform** | PinkRoccade / Ecare | Life-story field exists per librarian brief | Vendor pages confirm life-story + Omaha care plan | Field name and family-portal name unknown |
26	| **MedMij-certified PGOs** | Ivido, Quli, Drimpy, Patient1, etc. | Patient-controlled health environment with life-story support per MedMij standard | MedMij is the national framework; Caren itself is MedMij-compliant | Schema for life-story / preferences may or may not be standardised — needs investigation |
27	
28	### Unknown and worth a focused librarian pass
29	
30	1. **Does Nedap publish an API/import path** for *Mijn verhaal*, or is it strictly user-facing? If user-facing only, our v1 adapter is a "structured-export PDF/markdown that the patient or family can paste in," not a programmatic write.
31	2. **PinkRoccade family portal** — what is their family-facing equivalent of Caren? What is the field name for the life-story slot?
32	3. **MedMij data schema** — is there a defined data structure for "life story" / "care preferences" / "ACP topics"? If yes, we structure our output to match.
33	4. **Hospital ECDs (HiX, Epic NL, Nexus)** — do any have patient-voice / personhood slots, or is hospital admission always a standalone-UI moment?
34	5. **VVT-side standards** — does Vilans / Waardigheid en Trots publish a recommended schema for *persoonsbeeld* / *levensverhaal* that any ECD could adopt?
35	
36	## Implications for the demo
37	
38	- The prototype should make the dual mode visible:
39	  - **Standalone view** — the in-house care-passport UI (already planned).
40	  - **Adapter export** — a clearly-labelled "Export to Caren / *Mijn verhaal*" button that produces a structured markdown/PDF document mapped to the six ACP themes plus personhood + care-execution preferences. Even if the actual write is manual paste in v1, *showing the export shape* is the pitch moment.
41	- Seed transcripts should produce passports that obviously fill the *Mijn verhaal* slot better than a free-text family note would. The contrast is the demo.
42	- The pitch slide for distribution: "We meet you where you already work. Caren day one. PinkRoccade and MedMij PGOs next. Standalone for everyone else."
43	
44	## Implications for safety / boundaries
45	
46	- **Consent** must explicitly cover "export to your ECD's family portal." The patient (or representative) authorises which surface receives the passport.
47	- **Provenance** must survive the export. The rendered *Mijn verhaal* content needs to retain or link to the source quotes; we lose our trust story otherwise.
48	- **No silent overwrites.** When updating an existing *Mijn verhaal* entry, the adapter shows a diff and asks for human sign-off before pushing.
49	
50	## Risks this decision creates
51	
52	- **Platform risk concentrates on Nedap.** If Nedap extends *Mijn verhaal* themselves with structured/voice capture, our adapter advantage shrinks. Mitigation: ship the structured-output spec fast, build the MedMij PGO export early, and make the "engine, not the field" framing dominant.
53	- **API gating.** If Nedap and PinkRoccade do not expose write APIs, our v1 is paste-based. That's fine for the pitch but creates a friction moat we'll need to break later via partnership or PGO route.
54	- **Standalone UI scope creep.** It's tempting to make it good enough that customers stay there. Resist. The standalone is a fallback, not a product line.
